claim: OpenClaw is an open-source local AI framework that learns from user interactions and can be customized with personality files (identity.md and soul.md)
actionable: Use OpenClaw to build a personalized local AI assistant; define personality through identity.md (who you are) and soul.md (how it communicates)

claim: OpenClaw can automatically build custom CRM systems by ingesting Gmail, Calendar, and meeting transcripts to maintain relationship history with vector search capability
actionable: Set up a CRM workflow that automatically: pulls Fathom meeting transcripts, matches attendees to contacts, extracts action items, and stores in SQLite with vector embeddings for natural language search

claim: Building a personal knowledge base that vectorizes and indexes any content (URLs, videos, tweets, PDFs) enables semantic search across all information you've consumed
actionable: Create a RAG system where you drop links into a Telegram topic; extract both article and tweet content, embed with vectors, and enable natural language queries

claim: A business advisory council using parallel independent AI experts that analyze multiple business data sources and synthesize recommendations runs better than single-expert analysis
actionable: Implement parallel agents with different expertise (financial, marketing, growth); have them analyze the same data set independently then synthesize findings

claim: Nightly security reviews of an entire codebase by AI agents analyzing for offensive/defensive/privacy concerns can surface real vulnerabilities before they cause issues
actionable: Schedule nightly automated security reviews using 4 different AI perspectives (offense, defense, privacy, operational); have Claude 4.6 summarize critical findings immediately

claim: Prompt injection is a major attack vector for local AI systems - protection requires deterministic code to sanitize external data before LLM processing
actionable: When building AI systems, use deterministic code to detect and sanitize prompt injection attempts before data reaches the LLM; treat all external web content as potentially malicious

claim: Token counting and cost tracking should be built into every LLM application to understand actual spend and optimize prompt efficiency
actionable: Implement token counting for every LLM call; track costs by agent/workflow; use this data to identify and fix prompt bloat before it becomes expensive

claim: Encrypted database backups to cloud storage should happen hourly for any local AI system using production data
actionable: Set up hourly encrypted tar archives of all SQLite databases with 7-day retention; sync git commits hourly; alert immediately on backup failures

claim: Vector embeddings alone are insufficient for table selection in databases - simple string matching combined with embeddings performs 100x better
actionable: When filtering tables for database queries, combine word matching (check if table name appears in query) with embeddings; word matching is much more reliable for exact matches

claim: Sub-agents that run background work in parallel keep main conversation responsive and allow complex tasks to complete without blocking
actionable: When using Claude/LLMs for complex tasks, delegate to background sub-agents for medium/major work while keeping the main conversation thread responsive

claim: AI systems can learn dietary patterns from food photos and symptom reports to identify food triggers with reasonable accuracy
actionable: For health tracking, combine image analysis of food with self-reported symptoms; let AI find patterns across time that might not be obvious manually
